name: Physics Push Pull
description: Players move physical objects by pushing, pulling, grabbing, or dragging them through collision-aware interaction.
design_notes: Push and pull mechanics need a clear manipulation transaction: acquire an object, validate mass and grip anchor, constrain movement, resolve collisions, then release or cancel cleanly. Designers should define when the player is locked, how slopes and corners behave, and what happens when the object blocks progress or leaves valid space.
design_purpose: Let players solve spatial puzzles by repositioning crates, blocks, carts, or props.
Make heavy objects feel distinct from inventory items or instant interactions.
Create collaboration between character movement, collision, and environmental layout.
player_fantasy: The player feels physically connected to an object and can use effort and positioning to reshape the space.

parameters.name: manipulable_body_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the object being pushed, pulled, grabbed, or constrained.

parameters.name: max_object_mass_kg
parameters.description: Maximum mass the actor can manipulate before requiring upgrades, multiple players, or alternate tools.

parameters.name: grip_anchor_offset
parameters.typical_range: body-local offset near handle or face center
parameters.description: Object-local anchor used to align character hands, pulling direction, and constraint attachment.

parameters.name: push_acceleration
parameters.description: Acceleration applied while the actor drives the object through the manipulation constraint.

parameters.name: alignment_tolerance_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed angle between actor facing, grip anchor, and desired movement direction before interaction fails.

edge_cases: The object wedges against a corner while the player remains locked to the manipulation state.
A moving platform, elevator, or slope changes the object frame of reference during push or pull.
Two players or AI actors attempt to manipulate the same body from different anchors.
A save file loads while the object is mid-constraint and the player is no longer nearby.
common_bugs: The player capsule overlaps the object after release and is pushed through walls by collision correction.
Client prediction moves the object locally but the server rejects the manipulation because mass or ownership changed.
Push acceleration ignores object mass and makes small props and heavy blocks move identically.
The grip anchor is not updated after rotation, so pull direction flips or drifts around corners.
balancing_notes: Manipulation pace should respect object weight without forcing long, repetitive pushes across empty rooms.
Grid-based pushing is easier to read for puzzles; freeform physics allows more emergence but needs stronger recovery tools.
Heavy-object limits can gate progression, but the required upgrade or partner state should be obvious.
accessibility_notes: Offer hold-to-push, toggle-grab, or simplified alignment options for players who struggle with sustained inputs.
Use clear object highlights and invalid-movement cues so players can understand why a push or pull is blocked.

implementation_notes.unity: Use Rigidbody constraints, ConfigurableJoint, or a custom kinematic motor depending on whether emergence or puzzle precision is more important. Store manipulable_body_id, grip_anchor_offset, actor owner, and transaction state; release through one path that resolves overlaps and server correction.
implementation_notes.godot: RigidBody with joints can support freeform motion, while CharacterBody-driven blocks work better for tile puzzles. Keep manipulation state separate from input state, validate mass and alignment before acquisition, and debug draw grip anchors plus blocked collision normals.
implementation_notes.unreal: Physics Handle Components, constraints, or custom movement modes can implement grabbing and pushing. Multiplayer projects should let the server own object motion, replicate constraint owner and body transform, and reject competing manipulation requests deterministically.
implementation_notes.web: For Phaser or Matter.js, use a constraint or kinematic target for dragged bodies and resolve player collision separately. Store object ownership and anchor data in the simulation model so replay, undo, or save/load can restore mid-puzzle state safely.

relationships.reason: Push and pull physics gives sliding-block puzzles stable object movement and blocked-path resolution.
Movable physics objects can trigger pressure plates through mass, contact, and enter-exit ordering.
Shared physics manipulation needs authority and correction rules to avoid divergent object positions.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses physics object manipulation as part of traversal, combat, and puzzles.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
example_games.note: Uses pushable blocks as spatial puzzle elements.

example_games.title: Portal 2
example_games.note: Uses carried and moved physical objects as part of puzzle setup.
